terms.definition: A public-health classification denoting chronic social isolation with morbidity risk comparable to smoking or obesity
Software that auto-generates interpersonal messages via large language models to maximize online match rates
terms.first_use_section: The technology has reached its conclusion

terms.definition: Degree to which relational ties within a population exhibit dependable, reciprocally binding interactions
Externally imposed limitations that shrink an agent’s option-set, thereby elevating average social coherence
The self-imposed acceptance of limits—e.g., monogamy, screen-time caps—absent external enforcement
A localized pocket where an ordered phase forms and grows, here used metaphorically for emerging community clusters
